name: PrimeRecovery
description:
Library component. Models the proposed gastown #3587 fix: `gt prime` checks parked work on agent startup and self-heals when the gate has already closed. Observes Polecat (looking for Parked) and Gate (looking for Closed); when both conditions hold, fires Wake to the polecat. Single-shot.

Real gastown intent: insert a `checkParkedWork(ctx RoleContext)` call in `runPrime()` (internal/cmd/prime.go) that mirrors `checkSlungWork`. When any agent starts and has parked work whose gate has been resolved, it auto-resumes regardless of whether the wake mail was successfully delivered.

This component is composed only into the FIX spec (gate-3587-fix.kinner.json). The BUG spec (gate-3587-bug.kinner.json) omits it -- when Deacon crashes mid-protocol there's nothing to self-heal and Polecat stays Parked.

POLECAT_OBSERVE.description: observe the polecat's lifecycle state to detect it's parked.

GATE_OBSERVE.description: observe the gate's state to detect the dependency was already resolved.

WAKE_OUT.description: send Wake to the polecat when self-heal conditions are met. Tagged 'Wake'.

actors.name: PrimeRecovery
actors.owner: Single-shot self-heal sender. Fires Wake once when (Polecat=Parked AND Gate=Closed).

triples.event: self-heal: parked polecat + closed gate detected; send wake
